Hi Ana. So, this afternoon we deployed the build to staging, and the login is fine now.
Sorry Rahul, could you say that again, a little slower? I want to get every word.
Of course. We deployed the new build to the staging server. The login page works now.
Got it. What do you mean by staging? Is that the test server?
Yes, exactly. Staging is the test server, before production.
Okay. And the checkout? So you are saying it still has a bug?
Right. The test failed twice, so please do not move it to production yet.
Understood. Let me confirm: login is fine, checkout is not ready. I will send a summary.
Sorry, could you say that again, a little slower?
So you are saying the test failed twice, right?
Could you spell that name for me, please?
I did not catch the last part. Could you repeat it?

Hi Ana, this is Rahul from the Bangalore team. Thanks for the call earlier. I want to leave a quick summary, so nothing is lost. This afternoon, we deployed the new build to the staging server, and the login page is working well now. But the checkout still shows one bug on slow connections. The test failed twice this morning, so please do not move it to production yet. One more thing: if my accent is fast on the call, just stop me and ask me to repeat. I really do not mind. Can you confirm the plan by end of day? Thanks a lot, and talk soon.

Hi Ana, it is Priya, your team lead. Here are three quick tips for calls with any accent. First, do not try to catch every single word. Listen for the main idea, and let the small words go. Second, use the context. If you miss one word, the sentence around it usually tells you the meaning. Third, and most important, just ask. A clarifying question is not a weakness; it is a professional skill. Say 'Could you repeat that?' or 'What do you mean by that word?' Everyone does it, even native speakers. One last thing: after a few minutes, your ear adjusts to a new accent, so give it time. You are doing great.

First, Ana joined the video call with Rahul from the Bangalore team. Then she listened for the main idea and did not stop at every word. Next, when she misheard a detail, she used the context to guess it. After that, she asked a clarifying question and asked Rahul to slow down. Finally, she confirmed the important points before the call ended.
